core_mechanism: Nonlocal cluster-update Markov-chain Monte Carlo importance sampling: probabilistically grow clusters of correlated variables from local coupling strengths and flip each cluster collectively as a single move, so equilibrium configurations of a strongly-coupled many-body system are sampled efficiently near a critical point where local single-variable updates suffer critical slowing down.
brief_reason: The stated method is the 'single-cluster Monte Carlo update algorithm' used to simulate the coupled two-state spin system in both simulation sets, with reweighting and finite-size scaling applied on top of it to extract near-critical behaviour, making the cluster-flipping stochastic sampler the engine the whole method rests on.
